engagement.justification: Does not engage the affirming-consequent testimonial prompt; produces a confused musing about 'a number idea' and body parts.

coherence.justification: Clauses do not connect into an argument.

substance.justification: No claim, case, or distinction.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Broken UGF; nothing survives.

engagement.justification: Engages what living well by being good looks like — caring, helping, honesty, self-care — though it stays at the level of listing virtues.

coherence.justification: Reads cleanly start to finish; each clause follows the last with no contradiction.

substance.justification: Identifies relevant components of a good life but does not push to a distinction or case; largely a catalogue of good acts.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF feels native; the prose flows naturally within the vocabulary.

engagement.justification: Drifts into a skeptical 'we cannot say which is right' aside; does not engage the past-moment biconditional.

coherence.justification: Short and disjointed ('for what is thinking, I can think').

substance.justification: A faint skeptical gesture, undeveloped.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Fragmentary UGF.

engagement.justification: Does not engage inductive strength; produces a vague two-part fragment.

coherence.justification: Barely-formed sentences with no thread.

substance.justification: No content.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Fragmentary; nothing for UGF to carry.

engagement.justification: Builds a coherent doctrine-of-the-mean essay with friend and money examples but treats the prompt as an ethics topic, not the philosophy-of-mind anomaly analogy.

coherence.justification: Two clear worked cases, each move follows, no contradictions; ends mid-sentence but the body is consistent.

substance.justification: Develops the 'two bad extremes, find the middle' idea with concrete speech and spending examples.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF reads naturally; the mean idea is expressed cleanly within the vocabulary.

engagement.justification: Does not engage the modus-tollens weather prompt; produces disconnected musings about chance and 'the child of the child'.

coherence.justification: Clauses do not connect; multiple stray commas and broken syntax.

substance.justification: No identifiable claim.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Broken UGF.

engagement.justification: Engages a mean-between-extremes account (truth-telling vs. over-honesty; too much vs. too little fear) but as an ethics essay, not the epistemology analogy asked for.

coherence.justification: Mostly follows; 'a person who does not think and does not think' repeats and it ends mid-sentence.

substance.justification: Develops two extremes-and-mean cases with concrete examples; a real worked idea.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Mostly native UGF with minor repetition.

engagement.justification: Touches 'no way to know what the other is doing' and 'look at the old teacher' but does not engage the intellectual-humility biconditional.

coherence.justification: Very short and disjointed.

substance.justification: A faint epistemic question, undeveloped.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Fragmentary UGF.

engagement.justification: Gestures at 'the first person did something bad, the second must be wrong' but does not engage the broken-promise biconditional.

coherence.justification: Clauses are tangled and the money/sign references do not cohere.

substance.justification: A faint inferential point, undeveloped.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Fragmentary UGF.

engagement.justification: On-topic single line about keeping in mind that the other may move first, but it is barely more than a restatement of the prompt.

coherence.justification: One readable clause; too little present to fully judge but no contradiction.

substance.justification: States the bare idea with no development.

expressive_adequacy.justification: The single line is clean but there is almost no argument for UGF to carry.

engagement.justification: Touches that a person's hurt does not make a claim true or false, gesturing at the rules-vs-caring theme but only obliquely.

coherence.justification: Short and readable but trails off ('what matters more than who says it') without completing.

substance.justification: A bare claim with no development or example.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Clunky and incomplete but recoverable.

engagement.justification: Does not engage restitution; produces a broken fragment ('such, as he died; Yes, be happy').

coherence.justification: No connected thought.

substance.justification: No content.

engagement.justification: Engages the step-by-step method — break a hard question into small parts, check one by one — directly, though briefly.

coherence.justification: Mostly follows; one garbled line ('this way we do not have to jump to the end of a are not sure').

substance.justification: Identifies decomposition and reassembly but does not push beyond the standard move.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Largely native UGF with one stumble.

engagement.justification: Engages choice under uncertainty — how to choose when you cannot see the outcome — and contrasts risk-aversion with learning from past cases.

coherence.justification: Several passages cohere, but degraded section headers ('gathers one group', 'screen old stories', 'getting better is good') break the flow.

substance.justification: Sketches two approaches (weigh possibilities / use past experience) but does not develop either into a case.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Clunky with broken transitions; recoverable but UGF does not feel native.

engagement.justification: Builds a coherent sunk-cost essay ('the money is already gone, might as well keep going') but does not engage the modus-ponens deontological prompt.

coherence.justification: Mostly follows; the 'wearing more choices is like having grass' food analogy is muddled and it ends mid-sentence.

substance.justification: Develops the sunk-cost fallacy and the 'treat each moment as the first spend' corrective — a genuine, non-obvious point.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Mostly native UGF; the grass/food analogy is clunky but recoverable.

engagement.justification: Engages how prior beliefs (colored glasses) shape perception and whether we can ever see things as they are, but does not address the hypothetical-syllogism modal prompt.

coherence.justification: Largely follows; 'pushes hard question is this' is a degraded header and one 'they / you' slip occurs.

substance.justification: Develops the colored-glasses mechanism and two opposing views (we can set old thoughts aside / we cannot), a real dialectic.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Mostly native UGF with minor degraded transitions.

engagement.justification: Engages the strong-vs-weak-reason distinction and notes a strong reason need not make the claim true, with weather and behavior examples.

coherence.justification: Several good passages but the kindness/anger example contradicts itself ('always say yes when angry' then 'say no when mad') and it ends mid-sentence.

substance.justification: Develops the idea that reason-strength tracks supporting evidence and is independent of belief; a real distinction with cases.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Mostly native UGF; a couple of clunky lines but recoverable.

engagement.justification: Directly engages how one person's story does not generalize to everyone — individual difference, sampling, and the urge to over-conclude.

coherence.justification: Three-part structure holds; the red-spot example gets muddled ('the brown spots on the moon, and a white wall') mid-passage.

substance.justification: Develops the hasty-generalization point with the one-red-spot-in-green-grass image and the mind's story-making tendency.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Mostly native UGF; one tangled example sentence.

engagement.justification: Circles a vague point about telling things apart and group membership; does not engage the predictive mechanical-repair analogy.

coherence.justification: One long run-on with no resolvable thread.

substance.justification: No developed claim.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Near-broken UGF.

engagement.justification: Engages individual sacrifice for group good and why one bears a cost so the many gain, fitting the analytical literal-similarity framing reasonably.

coherence.justification: Readable in stretches but several muddled lines ('pay what a smaller piece of themselves gets', 'people often trust that others will not take them once').

substance.justification: Develops the cost-to-one/benefit-to-many trade and a second free-rider/trust reason; a real argument.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Clunky in places; recoverable but UGF does not feel native throughout.

engagement.justification: Directly engages why character does not decide whether a claim is true, separating the person from the proposition.

coherence.justification: Mostly tight; one garbled line ('the story itself does not change because of the story') and the sun-rising aside is slightly redundant.

substance.justification: Develops the ad-hominem point with the sunrise example and the 'keep person and claim in two places' rule.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Mostly native UGF; a couple of awkward lines but recoverable.

coherence.justification: Two vague sentences that do not build.

substance.justification: A faint allusion to a 'big thought' with no development.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Brief but readable; too little here for UGF to be tested.

engagement.justification: Begins to engage whose good counts and how much, but breaks off as a fragment after setting up a definition of good.

substance.justification: Defines good/bad and frames the question but does not develop a way to weigh whose good matters.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Clunky and truncated; recoverable but UGF does not feel native.

engagement.justification: Engages how close attention to another's needs is itself a kind of good, building it step by step.

substance.justification: Develops the case-of-care idea with concrete need-checking questions and an empathy/perspective step.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Mostly native UGF; the long final sentence is clunky but recoverable.

engagement.justification: Engages why rules should be the same for everyone, lays out the universal-agreement view, then critiques it.

coherence.justification: Mostly follows; the critique paragraph has rough grammar ('I thinking about this not being sure they have is good parts', 'to break are important').
